## UBS: Data Center Investment to Drive Demand for ANET, LITE, CLS, EXTR — Supply Constraints Remain Headwind

UBS framing demand as real but supply-constrained is the key tension for networking and optical names — ANET is the highest-conviction watchlist beneficiary, but the supply caveat limits near-term upside revision risk. Monitor whether constraints are a temporary bottleneck or a structural ceiling as AI capex ramps.

- UBS published a data center sector note naming ANET, CLS, EXTR, and LITE specifically in focus.
- The demand read is positive: data center investment is expected to drive revenues across networking and optical names.
- Supply constraints are flagged as a persistent offset to the demand tailwind — net framing is mixed-to-constructive.
- Coverage spans both networking (ANET, EXTR) and optical adjacency (LITE), reflecting broad sector positioning rather than single-name conviction.
